title: Crispy Cheese & Marmite Gnocchi
summary: This 7-ingredient gnocchi is super affordable, comes together in 15 minutes and hits all of the comfort food notes. We got all the ingredients for this recipe from Deliveroo.

method.description: Bring a pot of water to a boil. Season with salt and add your gnocchi. Cook for 2 mins then remove with a slotted spoon, saving the water.
Finely slice the leeks and spring onions. Separate the whites of the spring onions from the greens.
Heat a large non-stick frying pan over a high heat, add a glug of oil and your blanched gnocchi. Cook for 5 mins, tossing regularly until golden brown and crispy. Remove from the pan onto a plate.

Add another glug of oil and chuck in your leeks and the whites of the spring onion. Cook over a medium heat, stirring regularly, with a small pinch of salt. Cook until the leeks are a pale green and have sweated down, you don’t want too much colour, this should take around 5 mins.

Add the cream cheese and a big ladle of the gnocchi cooking water. Stir until loose and creamy. Add the Marmite and stir until incorporated. Cook until bubbling again, then grate in three-quarters of the cheddar.

Add the gnocchi and a big handful of spring onion greens to the pan. Toss in with plenty of freshly cracked pepper and lemon juice, and have a taste. Adjust the seasoning, adding a little more gnocchi water if the mix is too tight.
Serve with extra cheddar, black pepper and spring onion greens. Get stuck in.

chefs.name: Ben Lippett
chefs.summary: Ben Lippett (@dinnerbyben) is a cook, food writer, recipe developer, and co-founder of Dr Sting's Hot Honey. Ben spent years working at a spate of restaurants in the UK and Australia – including a brief but rewarding stint at a butcher's – before making the leap to food media.
